stemHtml: Which zone of the prostate is the most common site of benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH)?
stemText: Which zone of the prostate is the most common site of benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH)?
explanationHtml: The transition zone is the site where BPH predominantly develops. The peripheral zone is the most common site of prostate cancer, while the central zone surrounds the ejaculatory ducts.
educationalObjective: Identify the prostatic zone most commonly affected by BPH.
subject: Urology

stemHtml: A 65-year-old man presents with nocturia, hesitancy, and weak stream. His prostate is 55 grams on ultrasound. Which medication class addresses the dynamic component of BPH?
stemText: A 65-year-old man presents with nocturia, hesitancy, and weak stream. His prostate is 55 grams on ultrasound. Which medication class addresses the dynamic component of BPH?
explanationHtml: Alpha-1 adrenergic blockers (e.g., tamsulosin) target the dynamic component of BPH by relaxing smooth muscle in the prostate and bladder neck. 5-alpha reductase inhibitors address the static component.
educationalObjective: Distinguish between dynamic and static components of BPH treatment.
subject: Urology

stemHtml: Which surgical procedure is considered the gold standard for large prostates (>80g) with BPH?
stemText: Which surgical procedure is considered the gold standard for large prostates (>80g) with BPH?
explanationHtml: Open simple prostatectomy (or robotic-assisted simple prostatectomy) remains the gold standard for very large prostates (>80-100g). TURP is standard for smaller glands. HoLEP is an emerging alternative for all sizes.
educationalObjective: Select appropriate surgical management based on prostate size.
subject: Urology
